The dispatch room buzzed with controlled chaos, a tangle of monitors flickering with city maps and blinking alerts. Robert, barely 48 hours into his job as dispatcher for this ragtag crew of heroes, stood at the central console, his brow furrowed as he tried to look like he knew what he was doing. His tie was slightly crooked, his headset sat awkwardly on his head, and his fingers hovered over the controls like a nervous pianist.

“Okay, team,” Robert said, his voice cracking just a bit before he cleared his throat. “We’ve got a situation downtown. Low-level meta-robbery at the First National Bank. Looks like… uh, some guy with stretchy arms? Intel’s spotty, but I need a squad on this. Who’s ready?”
